message:
Add reusable scroll-triggered animation components

Introduces two new animation components for enhancing the web UI with
scroll-triggered reveal effects. Both components use framer-motion with
optimized performance and accessibility considerations.

## CharacterReveal Component

Animates text character by character with various preset styles.

### Props
- `text`: The text to animate
- `preset`: Animation style ("typewriter" | "cascade" | "wave" | "glitch" | "elastic")
- `delay`: Initial delay before animation (seconds)
- `stagger`: Delay between each character (seconds)
- `once`: Whether to play animation only once
- `threshold`: Viewport intersection threshold (0-1)
- `gradient`: Preserve gradient styling on text
- `spring`: Custom spring physics config

### Preset Animations
- **typewriter**: Characters appear in sequence with fade
- **cascade**: Characters fall in from above with stagger
- **wave**: Characters animate with wave-like motion
- **glitch**: Glitchy reveal with random jitter
- **elastic**: Bouncy elastic entrance

### Usage
```tsx
<CharacterReveal
  text="Welcome to JeffreysPrompts"
  preset="cascade"
  delay={0.2}
  stagger={0.03}
/>
```

## ScrollReveal Component

Wraps any content with scroll-triggered reveal animations.

### Props
- `children`: Content to animate
- `preset`: Animation style (10 presets available)
- `delay`: Initial delay (seconds)
- `duration`: Animation duration (seconds)
- `once`: Play animation only once
- `threshold`: Viewport intersection threshold
- `stiffness/damping`: Spring physics customization
- `spring`: Use spring vs tween animation
- `disabled`: Disable animation (for reduced motion preference)

### Preset Animations
- **fade-up/down/left/right**: Directional fade entrances
- **fade-scale**: Fade in with scale
- **blur-in**: Fade in with blur clearing
- **slide-rotate**: Slide with rotation
- **zoom-blur**: Zoom in with blur
- **flip-up**: 3D flip from bottom
- **bounce-in**: Bouncy scale entrance

### Usage
```tsx
<ScrollReveal preset="fade-up" delay={0.1}>
  <PromptCard prompt={prompt} />
</ScrollReveal>
```

## Implementation Details

Both components use:
- `useInView` hook for viewport detection
- `motion` components for GPU-accelerated animation
- TypeScript for full type safety
- Sensible defaults for easy use
- Custom spring physics support
- `cn()` utility for className merging

## Accessibility

- `once={true}` default prevents animation loops
- `disabled` prop for respecting reduced motion preferences
- Animations are subtle and purposeful
- No essential content hidden during animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
